title: Standardised soil profile data to support global mapping and modelling (WoSIS snapshot 2019)
authors: Niels H. Batjes; Eloi Ribeiro; Ad van Oostrum
topic: official global soil profile data / harmonisation
method_summary: Harmonises disparate soil profile observations into a standardised global soil profile snapshot suitable for mapping and modelling.
relevance_note: Best methodological reference for using ISRIC WoSIS as the Indonesia soil backbone.

title: Spectral data of tropical soils using dry-chemistry techniques (VNIR, XRF, and LIBS): A dataset for soil fertility prediction
authors: Tiago Rodrigues Tavares; José Paulo Molin; Lidiane Cristina Nunes; Elton Eduardo Novais Alves; Francisco José Krug; Hudson Wallace Pereira de Carvalho
topic: tropical soil fertility dataset / feature engineering
method_summary: Publishes tropical soil lab measurements and spectral observations for fertility prediction, including clay, organic matter, CEC, pH, exchangeable P and K.
relevance_note: Useful feature reference for tropical soils even though it has no crop label.

title: Datasets of drought and flood impact on croplands in Southeast Asia from 1980 to 2019
method_summary: Publishes gridded and temporal PDSI-based drought/flood indicators for croplands in Southeast Asia, including country-specific CSVs and GIS layers.
relevance_note: Good regional climate-stress auxiliary source when Indonesia-specific climate layers are needed beyond normals.

title: Impacts of droughts and floods on croplands and crop production in Southeast Asia – An application of Google Earth Engine
authors: Manjunatha Venkatappa; Nophea Sasaki; Phoumin Han; Issei Abe
dataset_used: TerraClimate PDSI + cropland overlays; companion public dataset in Data in Brief
method_summary: Uses Google Earth Engine with drought/flood indicators and cropland layers to quantify climate impacts on agricultural land.
relevance_note: Useful methodological companion to the downloaded SEA PDSI dataset.

title: Incorporating soil information with machine learning for crop recommendation to improve agricultural output
authors: Hadeeqa Afzal; Madiha Amjad; Ali Raza; Kashif Munir; Santos Gracia Villar; Luis Alonso Dzul Lopez; Imran Ashraf
topic: crop recommendation ML with soil variables
dataset_used: 2200-row NPK/pH/humidity/rainfall/temperature crop dataset from Pakistan via Kaggle and IEEE Dataport references
method_summary: Evaluates multiple ML models and an RF+XGB ensemble for crop recommendation using NPK, pH, humidity, rainfall and temperature.
relevance_note: Methodologically very close to the target task, but the article states the data must be requested rather than downloaded directly from the paper.

title: Machine Learning Based Recommendation of Agricultural and Horticultural Crop Farming in India Under the Regime of NPK, Soil pH and Three Climatic Variables
authors: Biplob Dey; Jannatul Ferdous; Romel Ahmed
topic: crop recommendation with NPK + soil pH + climate
dataset_used: benchmark crop recommendation data mentioned in paper
method_summary: Studies crop recommendation under NPK, soil pH and climatic variables, focusing on machine-learning model comparison.
relevance_note: Helpful methodological fit for feature selection and baseline framing, but not a dependable open-data source by itself.

title: A predictive analytics model for crop suitability and productivity with tree-based ensemble learning
authors: Isaac Kofi Nti; Adib Zaman; Owusu Nyarko-Boateng; Adebayo Felix Adekoya; Frimpong Keyeremeh
dataset_used: crop suitability and productivity dataset (not clearly exposed as open download in article metadata)
region_focus: generic / not clearly stated in accessible metadata
method_summary: Uses tree-based ensemble learning for crop suitability and productivity prediction.
relevance_note: Useful modelling reference when moving from recommendation to suitability scoring, but accessible metadata does not expose a reusable dataset.

title: Effect of Nitrogen, Phosphorus, and Potassium fertilizers on cocoa in Indonesia
authors: Nur S. Ruseani; Wouter Vanhove; Noto E. Prabowo; Mahadani Lubis; Patrick Van Damme
dataset_used: long-term cocoa NPK fertilizer response trial in East Java
method_summary: Summarises a long-term Indonesian cocoa trial showing strong nitrogen response and limited phosphorus/potassium response under the studied soil/management conditions.
relevance_note: Important domain reminder that nutrient-response patterns can be crop- and site-specific in Indonesia, but no open tabular dataset was released.
